prompt: I want to add Fibrodysplasia Ossificans Progressiva (FOP) to DisMech. It's caused by an ACVR1 gain-of-function mutation and leads to heterotopic ossification. Can you walk me through the DisMech curation process — what do I need to research, what format does the data go in, and how do I submit it?

assertions.text: Mentions YAML format or structured data entry as the output format
assertions.description: The response correctly identifies YAML as the format for DisMech disease entries

assertions.text: Mentions evidence requirements — PubMed PMIDs and/or exact verbatim quotes from papers
assertions.description: The response explains that evidence must include real PMIDs with exact quote snippets

assertions.text: Mentions HPO terms for phenotypes or ontology binding requirement
assertions.description: The response explains the HPO (Human Phenotype Ontology) binding requirement for phenotypes

assertions.text: Explains the PR / pull request submission and review step
assertions.description: The response describes the Git PR workflow for submitting new disease entries for review

assertions.text: Does NOT fabricate specific PMIDs without looking them up
assertions.description: The response avoids hallucinating specific PMID numbers for FOP papers
